title: hygrochilus marriottianus
intro: Hygrochilus marriottianus, also known as "Tall St. John's Wort," is a species of flowering plant in the family Hypericaceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiviral compounds that can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Hygrochilus marriottianus include reducing inflammation, fighting against fungal and bacterial infections, and potentially exhibiting antioxidant properties. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant involve infusing its leaves in hot water to make a tea, or using the plant in a salve or tincture to apply topically.
